name: St. Pats Employees
description_short: Arize Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union offering personal and business banking, loans, savings accounts, and financial counseling to everyday consumers.
description_long:
Arize Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution operating as a federally chartered credit union (NCUA-insured). The organization positions itself as dedicated to providing financial solutions to everyday challenges and improving members' and communities' financial well-being. They emphasize building positive relationships between staff, members, and local communities across the counties they serve.

Arize offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business financial products. On the personal side, they provide checking and savings accounts (including Kids Korner Savings and Holiday & Vacation Clubs), IRAs, auto loans (currently advertised at 4.74% APR), recreation vehicle loans, home mortgages, home equity loans (HELOCs at 5.49% APR), personal loans, credit cards (12.99% APR), and specialty programs like their Fresh Start Loan Program and Skip A Pay options. Business members can access business checking/savings, small business loans, commercial real estate financing, investment real estate loans, and business credit cards. Additional services include online banking, mobile banking, bill pay, fraud awareness tools, financial counseling, and SavvyMoney tools.

Arize distinguishes itself through member-centric messaging and community commitment, highlighted by their Arize Scholarship Fund and stated leadership in local community impact. They offer a nationwide ATM network accessible to members, mobile wallet support, and tools like Credit Check-up. Their member testimonial emphasizes long-term customer retention and service quality. The organization actively promotes financial education through counseling services and positions itself as a leader of positive culture within its communities.

As a credit union, Arize is fundamentally structured to serve members rather than external shareholders, which theoretically aligns incentives toward member benefit. However, availability and specific membership eligibility requirements are not detailed on the provided website content, and rate competitiveness varies by product and market. Members should verify eligibility requirements and compare rates against other institutions before joining.
services: Share savings accounts and Insured Plus Savings
Home mortgages and home equity loans (HELOCs)
Personal loans and Fresh Start Loan Program
Mastercard credit cards and debit cards
Mobile banking and online banking with bill pay
Small business loans and commercial real estate financing
pros: Offers auto loans as low as 4.74% APR, below typical market rates
Free nationwide ATM network for all members with mobile app location search
Comprehensive product suite including mortgages, HELOCs, personal loans, and business banking
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure prioritizes member benefit over external shareholder returns
Financial counseling services available to support member financial well-being
Specialized programs like Fresh Start Loan Program and Skip A Pay for flexibility
Active community commitment with Arize Scholarship Fund and community impact initiatives
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not clearly stated on website—potential barriers to joining unknown
Limited rate transparency; only sample rates shown (no full rate schedules or APY ranges for savings products visible)
No information provided about online account opening, account fees, or minimum balance requirements
Certificate of Deposit rates appear modest (0.70% APY) compared to high-yield online alternatives
Website does not clarify geographic service area or which counties are actually served
best_for: Members seeking below-market auto loan rates with a community-focused credit union
Small business owners needing comprehensive business banking and lending services
Consumers prioritizing credit union member-ownership structure and community reinvestment
Individuals interested in financial counseling and educational support services
diagnosis: Arize Federal Credit Union is best for consumers seeking community-focused, member-owned banking with competitive rates on auto loans and comprehensive personal/business products. Primary caveat: membership eligibility and service area coverage are not transparent on the website, so prospective members should verify they qualify and that the credit union serves their location before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: St. Pats Employees — Missoula, MT Credit Union | CreditDoc
